cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Game runs alright at full speed. The sound is slow and low quality, having lots of crackles. This may be solved with some wine/protontricks, but I haven't tested yet. I'd say xact (since it has xaudio) could solve it, bringing it to Gold. Tested: initial area, a couple battles.
title: Epic Battle Fantasy 5

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: I needed to follow the instructions to change my pulseaudio settings under the section "optimize for steam games". Basically changing sample rates and priorities. (https://ludwig.im/en/projects/steam-pulseaudio-sound-latency-lagging-problem-noise#optimize-pulseaudio-settings-for-steam-games) Now the game runs with perfect music. I'll need to test longer to see if I have any crash, but it seems great so far!
title: Epic Battle Fantasy 5

notes: Have Linux support :D but Proton Work Great
title: Epic Battle Fantasy 5

notes: Crackly sound, otherwise seemed to run fine.
title: Epic Battle Fantasy 5
